raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Speaker Robert Rivas announced a new rule that reduces the number of bills Assemblymembers can introduce from 50 to 35 over the 2025-26 Legislative Session.",
    "Reducing the bill limit will allow legislators to invest additional energy on issues that matter most to Californians.",
    "The new bill limit is expected to improve legislative efficiency and ensure a more focused approach to lawmaking.",
    "By lowering the bill limit, legislators can devote more time to evaluating each proposal and engage in meaningful debate.",
    "The reduced bill load will provide more opportunities for thoughtful deliberation and greater oversight of government spending and programs.",
    "The change in bill limit is expected to prioritize quality over quantity in legislative efforts.",
    "The decision to focus on critical issues such as housing affordability, public safety, and protecting vulnerable communities is part of a shift toward effective governance.",
    "Setting a reasonable bill limit can lead to more intentional legislation that serves the people of California."
  ]
}
```
